2606.23879.title: Promise and challenges of heart chamber segmentation from non-contrast CT scans using contrastive unpaired image translation: a feasibility study
2606.23879.paper_summary: Purpose: To evaluate the feasibility and challenges of heart chamber segmentation from non-contrast CT scans using contrastive unpaired image translation and deep learning-based segmentation. Approach: We developed ChameleonNet, a framework utilizing the Contrastive Unpaired Translation (CUT) network with decoupled contrastive learning (DCL) loss to synthesize non-contrast CT from contrast CT scans. Using annotations of four heart chambers (left atrium (LA), left ventricle (LV), right atrium (RA), and right ventricle (RV)) from contrast scans, we trained a Hausdorff distance loss-enhanced nnU-Net on synthesized non-contrast images. The translation model was trained with 35,538 contrast-enhanced and 37,197 non-contrast CT slices. The segmentation model was trained with 292 synthesized non-contrast scans. Performance was evaluated using Dice similarity coefficient (DSC) and 95th Hausdorff distance (HD95) on 36 synthesized non-contrast scans, and volume agreement on 36 real non-contrast CT scans was assessed using Pearson correlation, mean absolute percentage error (MAPE), and mean percentage error (MPE). Results: The segmentation model achieved DSC of 0.94 (0.01), 0.91 (0.04), 0.92 (0.03), 0.93 (0.02), and HD95 of 3.63 (1.49), 5.74 (4.08), 5.18 (1.77), 5.51 (3.21) mm on synthesized non-contrast images for LA, LV, RA, and RV, respectively. On real non-contrast CT scans, Pearson correlations were 0.93, 0.82, 0.87, and 0.89 (all p<0.001), with MAPE ranging from 9.22% to 20.79%, and MPE ranging from -12.52% to 4.67%. Conclusions: ChameleonNet demonstrated feasibility for heart chamber segmentation from non-contrast CT without manual non-contrast annotations. However, volume errors, particularly for LV and RV, indicate that further refinement and validation are needed before clinical use.
2606.23879.paper_summary_zh: 目的：評估從非對比CT掃描中進行心臟腔室分割的可行性和挑戰，使用對比無配對影像轉換和基於深度學習的分割方法。方法：我們開發了ChameleonNet，一個利用對比無配對轉換（CUT）網絡和解耦對比學習（DCL）損失來合成非對比CT的框架。使用來自對比掃描的四個心臟腔室（左心房（LA）、左心室（LV）、右心房（RA）和右心室（RV））的標註，我們在合成的非對比影像上訓練了一個增強Hausdorff距離損失的nnU-Net。轉換模型使用35,538個增強對比CT和37,197個非對比CT切片進行訓練。分割模型則在292個合成的非對比掃描上進行訓練。性能評估使用Dice相似係數（DSC）和第95百分位Hausdorff距離（HD95）在36個合成的非對比掃描上進行，並使用Pearson相關性、平均絕對百分比誤差（MAPE）和平均百分比誤差（MPE）評估36個真實非對比CT掃描的體積一致性。結果：分割模型在合成的非對比影像上對LA、LV、RA和RV的DSC分別達到0.94（0.01）、0.91（0.04）、0.92（0.03）、0.93（0.02），HD95分別為3.63（1.49）、5.74（4.08）、5.18（1.77）、5.51（3.21）毫米。在真實的非對比CT掃描中，Pearson相關性為0.93、0.82、0.87和0.89（所有p<0.001），MAPE範圍從9.22%到20.79%，MPE範圍從-12.52%到4.67%。結論：ChameleonNet展示了從非對比CT進行心臟腔室分割的可行性，無需手動非對比標註。然而，體積誤差，特別是對於LV和RV，表明在臨床使用之前需要進一步的完善和驗證。
2606.23879.author: Jing Wang et.al.
2606.23879.authors: Jing Wang, Tong Yu, Hao-En Lu, Zixue Zeng, Joseph K. Leader, Xin Meng, Jianbing Zhu, Jiantao Pu
